economic_notes: GBEs include Nuxalk Business Trust (100%), Nunumus Management Group (100%), and Bella Coola Hotel/0820770 B.C. Ltd. (100%). Nunumus Management Group had revenue of $6,973,687 and a net loss of $320,550. Bella Coola Hotel had revenue of $166,435 and net income of $73,692.
notes: Audit is qualified for three reasons: (1) Social housing TCA amortized at CMHC-required rate rather than useful life per PSAS; (2) PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations not yet assessed as of audit date; (3) GBE financial information not available for March 31, 2023 year-end, limiting scope. Three consolidated subsidiaries: Nuxalk Acwsalcmalslayc Academy of Learning Society, Nuxalk Nation Property Management, Nuxalk Nation Transition House Society. Three GBEs accounted for by modified equity method. No schedule of remuneration included in the financial statements. total_government_transfers calculated as ISC ($24,551,478) + FNHA ($3,607,626) + Province of BC ($5,039,376) + Mid Coast First Nations Training Society ($272,982) + Fisheries and Oceans ($328,540) + Coast Opportunity Funds ($388,238) + Vancouver Coastal Health ($126,050) + FNESC ($3,251,938) + CMHC ($102,647) = $37,668,875; osr = total revenue minus government transfers = $45,411,728 - $37,668,875 = $7,742,853. Note: Other contracts and government funding income ($6,314,415) may include additional government transfers not separately identified.
